title: Humboltia ochreata

Currently, it is recognized as a member of the genus Humboldtia, within the family Sapindaceae. intro: Humboldtia ochreata, also known as the Yellow Trumpet Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to the Western Ghats of India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia ochreata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory infections and digestive disorders.
